reasoning: The Federal Reserve's Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) was an emergency lending facility introduced in 2023. It officially ceased issuing new loans on March 11, 2024. Because loans were capped at a one-year term, all outstanding loans matured and were completely repaid by March 2025, bringing the FRED series balance to exactly zero. The program has remained permanently at a zero balance for nearly a year as of January 2026. Restarting the program would require a severe new banking crisis and specific authorization from the Fed and Treasury under Section 13(3) — and historically, new crises often result in identically functioning but newly named facilities rather than reinstating old ones. Therefore, it is overwhelmingly unlikely that any new loans will be extended under the BTFP over the next 3 months, keeping the balance flat at 0.0. Due to slight horizon uncertainty over whether a sudden extreme crisis could force the Fed to reopen the exact same facility, probabilities marginally increase over time, but stay at the very minimum allowable floor of ~2-3%.
